campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on May 19, 2026 to discuss staffing challenges and upcoming community events. The council held a public hearing on personnel matters and addressed recruitment and retention efforts for city positions.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Boogie Festival happening this weekend
Public hearing on personnel and government code compliance

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to pursue for the next budget year. Once council picks its preferred approach, staff will provide further direction on implementing that choice.
keyTopics: Selecting sheriff service level options for FY 2026-27 budget
Council to identify near-term sheriff strategy
Staff to provide implementation direction based on council's choice

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve financial reports, sign multi-year service contracts, and authorize the town manager to apply for state grants and execute a paving construction contract. The council also adopted a list of projects for the next fiscal year funded by state grants.
keyTopics: Approved March 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized O'Grady Paving construction contract
Submitted grant applications for state funding
Adopted fiscal year 2026-27 project list
Approved five-year service agreements with vendors

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 16, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 16, 2026, to handle routine administrative business including approving committee records and making appointments to city boards. The council also addressed a settlement involving Gordon Biersch Brewing Company, approved the city's hotel tax district annual report and assessment plan, and authorized the purchase of property on South First Street.
keyTopics: Gordon Biersch Brewing Company government claim settlement
Appointments to city boards and commissions
San José Hotel Business Improvement District annual report and assessments for 2026-2027
Real property acquisition at 26-36 South First Street

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door meeting to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and to review two legal matters involving the city. One case involves a lawsuit with San Francisco Baykeeper and Sunnyvale in federal court, and the council also discussed potential future litigation risks facing the city.
keyTopics: City Attorney hiring decision
San Francisco Baykeeper lawsuit (federal court case)
Anticipated litigation exposure

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific business items discussed in the closed session were not disclosed to the public, as is typical for confidential council discussions.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting convened
Regular council meeting held
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 15, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting with legal counsel to discuss an anticipated lawsuit that the city may file. The council then reported back to the public about what was discussed in that private session.
keyTopics: One pending litigation case the city is considering initiating
Lobbyist registration requirements under city ordinance Chapter 2.100

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on June 23, 2026, available both in-person and online via Zoom. Residents could participate remotely and submit written comments through the city's online agenda system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format allowing remote participation via Zoom
Public comment submission through eComments tab on City Council Agenda page
In-person attendance option at council chambers

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and consider changes to how the city plans its federal housing and community development spending. The council also discussed updating its vacancy procedures and reviewed requests from individual council members for future work.
keyTopics: Update to the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan (Consolidated Plan)
Changes to the council vacancy ordinance
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. The entire project would be affordable housing, and the developer is requesting special approvals under state law to allow the building's size and density on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units on 0.43-acre lot at 450 Lytton Avenue
Six-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Request for state density bonus concessions and waivers
Environmental review status under California law
